message:
br-bd-12hh: execute printf_conformance.json through in-process + harness matrix

Extend crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/printf_conformance_conformance_test.rs
beyond schema-only validation: dispatch every fixture case that carries a
concrete expected_output through the shared execute_fixture_case() executor and
assert the observed impl_output matches the fixture exactly.

Iteration rules:
- Skip cases whose oracle is expected_output_bytes or expected_output_pattern
  (e.g. %p pointer addresses, %a hex floats with locale/format-dependent
  rendering); execution/byte validation for those belongs in a dedicated
  bytes/pattern test path.
- For mode == "both" fan out into {"strict", "hardened"} and run the executor
  in each mode; otherwise run only the declared mode.
- Panic with a useful context string on executor errors so fixture failures
  point at the failing case name + mode directly.

New imports (frankenlibc_fixture_exec::execute_fixture_case,
std::process::{Command, Stdio}) pull in the shared executor already used by
the sibling socket_ops / syscall harnesses; keeping the import list aligned
with the in-tree convention lets follow-up subprocess-isolation coverage plug
in without further import churn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
